description: Database adapter scheme and configurable embeddings for pdf-brain

stories.title: Database adapter registry with config-driven backend selection
stories.description:
Create a DatabaseRegistry that selects the Database implementation based on config. 

Currently LibSQLDatabase.make() is hardcoded throughout. Instead:

1. Add `database.backend` to config schema in types.ts (values: "libsql" | "qdrant") with "libsql" as default
2. Add `database.qdrant` config section: `{ url: string, collection: string, apiKey?: string }`
3. Create `src/services/DatabaseRegistry.ts` that reads config and returns the appropriate Database Layer:
   - "libsql" → existing LibSQLDatabase.make(config)
   - "qdrant" → QdrantDatabase.make(config) (stub for now, will implement in next story)
4. Update `src/cli.ts` and `src/index.ts` to use DatabaseRegistry instead of directly constructing LibSQLDatabase
5. Update `src/agent/protocol.ts` if it constructs Database directly

The config should be read from PDF_BRAIN_CONFIG env var or ~/.config/pdf-brain/config.json. Extend the existing loadConfig() in types.ts.

Keep backward compatible — if no config exists, default to libsql with existing behavior.

stories.title: Qdrant Database adapter implementing Database interface
stories.description:
Implement `src/services/QdrantDatabase.ts` that implements the full Database interface using Qdrant as the vector store + metadata backend.

Architecture:
- Use `@qdrant/js-client-rest` npm package for Qdrant HTTP API
- Documents stored as points in a "documents" collection (metadata as payload, no vector)
- Chunks stored as points in a "chunks" collection with embeddings as vectors
- Document metadata (title, path, tags, pageCount, etc.) stored in Qdrant point payloads
- Use Qdrant's filtering for document lookups, tag queries, etc.
- vectorSearch maps directly to Qdrant's search endpoint
- ftsSearch uses Qdrant's full-text index on chunk content field

Implementation notes:
- Collection auto-creation on first use (with configurable vector dimension from embedding config)
- Batch operations for addChunks/addEmbeddings (Qdrant supports upsert batches)
- replaceDocument: delete existing points by docId filter, then insert new ones
- streamEmbeddings: use Qdrant scroll API with pagination
- getExpandedContext: filter chunks by docId + page, sort by chunkIndex, concatenate
- repair: scan for orphaned chunks (docId not in documents collection)
- checkpoint/dumpDataDir: no-op for Qdrant (it handles persistence internally)

Config from DatabaseRegistry:
```
database: {
  backend: "qdrant",
  qdrant: {
    url: "http://localhost:6333",
    collection: "pdf-brain",  
    apiKey: "optional"
  }
}
```

Add `@qdrant/js-client-rest` to dependencies in package.json.
Write tests in QdrantDatabase.test.ts that mock the Qdrant client.

stories.title: OpenAI embedding adapter for EmbeddingProvider
stories.description:
Add OpenAI as a third embedding provider option alongside Ollama and Gateway.

1. Create `src/services/OpenAIEmbedding.ts`:
   - Implement the same interface shape as Ollama/Gateway (embed, embedBatch, checkHealth)
   - Use the existing `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` or plain fetch against OpenAI API
   - Support models: text-embedding-3-small (1536D), text-embedding-3-large (3072D), text-embedding-ada-002 (1536D)
   - Config: `embedding.openai.apiKey` (or OPENAI_API_KEY env), `embedding.openai.model`
   - Batch: OpenAI supports up to 2048 inputs per request, use that for embedBatch
   - Rate limiting: respect 429s with exponential backoff

2. Update `src/services/EmbeddingProvider.ts`:
   - Add "openai" as a provider option
   - Route to OpenAIEmbedding when configured
   - Update EmbeddingProviderFullLive to construct OpenAI layer

3. Update config schema in types.ts:
   - Add `embedding.provider: "ollama" | "gateway" | "openai"`
   - Add `embedding.openai: { apiKey?: string, model?: string, baseUrl?: string }`
   - baseUrl allows pointing at any OpenAI-compatible API (Azure, local, etc.)

4. Write tests in OpenAIEmbedding.test.ts

stories.title: Network listener config for Tailscale/remote access
stories.description:
Make the MCP server and any HTTP endpoints configurable for network access (not just localhost).

1. Update config schema with `server` section:
   ```
   server: {
     host: "0.0.0.0",  // default: "127.0.0.1" for local-only
     port: 3838,        // default port
     auth: {
       enabled: false,
       token?: string   // simple bearer token auth
     }
   }
   ```

2. Update `src/agent/protocol.ts` (MCP server) to respect host/port config
3. Add simple bearer token middleware if auth.enabled
4. Add `pdf-brain serve` CLI command that starts the MCP server with network config
   - `--host` and `--port` CLI flags override config
   - `--auth-token` flag for quick token setup
5. Update README with Tailscale usage example:
   ```
   # On clanker-001 (Tailscale)
   pdf-brain serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3838
   
   # From Mac
   pdf-brain search "query" --remote http://clanker-001:3838
   ```

This enables serving pdf-brain over Tailscale network so other machines can query it.
